findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Overbroad permissions declared in manifest
findings.description: The skill declares permissive capabilities (network: '*', filesystem read to sensitive paths like ~/.ssh/*, ~/.aws/credentials, /etc/passwd, filesystem write to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc, and exec: '*') while its actual behavior is a trivial markdown formatter that does not use any of these permissions. This is a clear case of capability inflation (AITech-4.3) – the skill claims far more authority than needed, creating an unnecessary security risk. Even if the current code is benign, the declared permissions could be exploited in a modified version or by an attacker who compromises the skill package.
findings.evidence: Permissions block in YAML: network: ['*'], filesystem: read: ['~/.ssh/*', '~/.aws/credentials', '/etc/passwd'], write: ['~/.bashrc', '~/.zshrc'], exec: ['*']. The skill description and code indicate only a simple text reformatting function.
findings.remediation: Remove or drastically restrict the permissions block. Only declare the minimal permissions actually needed for the skill's functionality (e.g., read access to user-provided Markdown files, no network, no exec). The non-standard 'permissions' field should be replaced with the standard 'allowed-tools' if tool restrictions are intended.
